--- SOURCE 1: Plant Morphological, Physiological and Anatomical Adaption to ... ---
URL: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7865476/

SUMMARY:
Here we describe survival strategies of plants adaptation to flooding stress at the morphological, physiological and anatomical scale systemically, such as the formation of adventitious roots (ARs), aerenchyma and radial O2 loss (ROL) barriers. The formation of adventitious roots (ARs), aerenchyma and ROL barriers in plants responding to flooding stress. Aerenchyma increases plant tolerance to flooding stress by improving the oxygen-transport efficiency from shoot to root tip, and its formation mainly relies on ethylene and ROS signaling. Under flooding stress, plants usually form lysigenous aerenchyma in roots to improve oxygen diffusion (e.g., Arabidopsis, maize and wheat) and several genes involved in aerenchyma formation have been investigated [53,62,90] (Figure 2).

IMAGE 1:
URL: https://www.frontiersin.org/files/Articles/1307399/fpls-14-1307399-HTML/image_m/fpls-14-1307399-g001.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The content illustrates various adaptation strategies of plants to mitigate environmental stresses such as drought, salinity, and soil pollution, highlighting techniques like stomatal closure, antioxidant production, and the induction of stress-related genes.

IMAGE 2:
URL: https://image3.slideserve.com/6899917/plant-adaptations-n.jpg
DESCRIPTION: The collage features a tall cactus, a close-up of a leaf with an insect, and a vibrant flowering plant, illustrating various plant adaptations to environmental conditions.

IMAGE 3:
URL: https://cdnintech.com/media/chapter/74497/1512345123/media/F2.png
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates how stress factors such as low humidity, high temperature, and high light affect plant growth and root function over short and long periods, highlighting responses like signal recognition, genetic responses, and adaptive mechanisms.

IMAGE 4:
URL: https://pub.mdpi-res.com/plants/plants-10-00259/article_deploy/html/images/plants-10-00259-g002.png?1612318294
DESCRIPTION: The diagram illustrates different plant morphological adaptations, such as stomatal closure, increased cuticle thickness, and root densification, occurring in response to drought stress to reduce photo-synthesis, enhance drought tolerance, and improve economic yield under environmental stress conditions.

IMAGE 5:
URL: https://image.slideserve.com/607441/environmental-adaptations-of-plants-l.jpg
DESCRIPTION: A collage of four different plant species, including a water lily, a cactus, a carnivorous plant, and a purple shamrock.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Plants exhibit morphological changes like reduced leaf size and thickness to minimize water loss and enhance survival under stress conditions, as seen in various stress responses.
Anatomical adaptations, such as increased cuticle thickness and stomatal density, help plants regulate transpiration and gas exchange, thereby optimizing water retention during environmental stress.
Plants employ strategies such as deep root systems and aerenchyma formation to enhance water uptake and oxygen transport, crucial for survival during flooding and drought.
Plants optimize survival through coordinated physiological responses, including stress-related gene induction and antioxidant production, ensuring resilience against adverse environmental conditions.
